oai_dc:dc.dc:title: A Message from the President
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Waskey, Lewis “Buddy”
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 51 Number 2 (April/May 2024).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.President’s Message Dear Members and Associates, As we commemorate the 50th anniversary of the American Federation of Aviculture, we reflect on half a century of invaluable support for aviculturists dedicated to bird keeping. This milestone represents a collaborative effort among our individual and commercial members, as well as affiliated organizations, all united in their commitment to face and surmount challenges. Together, we have influenced legislation at both state and federal levels and addressed various regulations and ordinances throughout the United States. Our collective success is rooted in ongoingeducation, shared amongst ourselves and with the public, enhancing our care and conservation efforts for birds. The advent of modern technology has vastly simplified the ways in which we communicate and educate. Our esteemed journal, WatchBird, continues to be a cornerstone of our educational efforts, available quarterly in print and archived digitally for wider accessibility, with resources readily accessible on the AFA website.A highlight of our educational endeavors is the annual Education Conference, which offers a unique platform for aviculturists, conservationists, and bird enthusiasts to connect, learn, and network. I personally find it the most enriching experience for fostering growth and collaboration within our community. We are excited to announce that our 50th Anniversary Educational Conference will take place September 5-7, 2024, in Costa Mesa, California, and we eagerly anticipate your participation.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 51 No. 2 (2024): Watchbird April/May 2024; 3
